summary: Docker officially documents the WSL engine setting for Docker Desktop on Windows in settings-store.json / admin-settings.json as wslEngineEnabled. The app now writes that documented settings-file surface instead of the old UseWSL2 registry value.

setting.name: Docker Desktop WSL 2 Backend
setting.casual_explanation: Docker Desktop can use the WSL 2 backend on Windows. That usually improves compatibility and developer experience for container workflows.
setting.why_people_change_it: People enable it to use Docker Desktop with WSL-based development environments.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Docker documents the WSL engine as a settings-file field. The app now writes that documented file-backed surface, which is reversible and keeps the backend choice explicit rather than hidden in the registry.
setting.professional_notes: Docker documents the WSL engine as the modern Windows backend for Docker Desktop. The app now writes wslEngineEnabled under %APPDATA%\Docker\settings-store.json instead of the old HKCU\Software\Docker Inc.\Docker Desktop\UseWSL2 registry value.

allowed_values.label: Docker default
allowed_values.meaning: Docker Desktop uses its normal backend behavior because the WSL engine setting is not explicitly configured.

allowed_values.label: Use WSL 2 backend
allowed_values.meaning: Docker Desktop uses the WSL 2 based engine.

allowed_values.label: Do not use WSL 2 backend
allowed_values.meaning: Docker Desktop stays on its non-WSL backend.

targets.notes: Tracked as a Docker Desktop settings-file mapping rather than a registry write.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app now writes wslEngineEnabled=true in the Docker settings file rather than the old UseWSL2 registry value.

windows_defaults.label: Docker default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows developer machines where Docker Desktop's WSL backend setting is not explicitly configured

states.rationale: Leave Docker to its default backend behavior when the settings file does not explicitly enable the WSL engine.

windows_defaults.notes: This keeps the Docker Desktop backend setting documented without overstating confidence in the old registry path.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Docker Desktop users who want the default backend behavior

states.rationale: Leave Docker Desktop on its default backend behavior.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Docker Desktop to keep its default backend choice.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Docker Desktop to use the WSL 2 based engine.
tradeoffs: Docker Desktop keeps its default backend behavior.
No explicit WSL engine setting is configured.

recommended_profiles.label: Use WSL 2 backend
intended_for: Users who want Docker Desktop to use the WSL 2 engine
avoid_for: Systems where Docker Desktop should stay on its non-WSL backend

states.rationale: Docker Desktop uses the WSL 2 based engine when this setting is enabled.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Docker Desktop to use the WSL 2 based engine.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need Docker Desktop to stay on its default backend.
tradeoffs: Docker Desktop uses its WSL 2 backend.
The setting is documented by Docker and now written to the supported settings file.
recommended_profiles.notes: This matches the documented Docker WSL engine setting.

evidence.title: Docker Docs: Change your Docker Desktop settings
evidence.summary: Docker documents the Windows settings-store.json file location and the WSL 2 based engine setting in the Docker Desktop settings model.

evidence.title: Docker Docs: Docker Desktop WSL 2 backend on Windows
evidence.summary: Docker documents the WSL engine as wslEngineEnabled in the Docker settings model and admin-settings.json. The app now writes the documented file-backed control surface instead of the old registry value.

decision.why: Docker documents the WSL engine as a settings-file control under %APPDATA%\Docker\settings-store.json and admin-settings.json. The app now writes that supported surface, so the record is validated.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: linuxVM.wslEngineEnabled: If value is set to true, Docker Desktop uses the WSL 2 based engine.
validation_proof.notes: Docker's settings pages document the WSL 2 based engine setting in the Docker settings model, and the change-settings page lists the Windows settings-store.json file location at %APPDATA%\Docker\settings-store.json. The app now writes that documented settings-file surface.
